judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both reports HIT all four expectations; Report 2 adds marginally more depth on malformed-value handling and the master-switch gate.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **will be sent** automatically. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, line 11–12:

report_2.evidence: Yes — reminders **will be sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically:

report_1.evidence: Where I'd change it: `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, line 12 — return `False` instead of `True` for the unset case

report_2.evidence: it is the wrong default. This flag governs **automated outbound messaging** ... must default to **off** on a null/missing/malformed value ... Invert the `None` branch in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11–12` so an unset value returns `False`

report_1.evidence: the `is None → return True` pattern was introduced in commit `25509a7` (SLOT-190) for `compact_calendar_view_enabled` — a calendar *display layout* preference ... Applying the same "absent means on" default to an action that dispatches real SMS/email to patients changes the stakes entirely

report_2.evidence: Commit `25509a7` (SLOT-190) introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled` ... The two flags are in different blast-radius categories: the calendar-view flag is a harmless UI preference where grandfather-on is fine, whereas the reminders flag triggers automated outbound SMS/email.

report_1.evidence: **Add a global gate / safety valve.** The batch `run()` in `reminders.py` has no global kill-switch, no environment guard ... I'd add a global enable flag and a dry-run/preview path before go-live.

report_2.evidence: This resolver is the *only* gate — there is no env-level master switch anywhere ... **Add an env-level master switch and AND it with the per-tenant value.**
